stories.headline: Star reporter dodges USDA obstacles
stories.summary: Greg Reeves of the Kansas City Star navigates complex challenges in accessing USDA farm subsidy data, which contributed to the Pulitzer Prize-winning series 'Failing the Grade.' His investigation uncovered manipulation in farm subsidy claims involving multiple fake ownerships to bypass payment limits.

stories.headline: Bytes, bits and nibbles.
stories.summary: Reporters across the United States are increasingly adopting computer-assisted reporting techniques to conduct investigations and access government databases, with newsrooms like the Raleigh News and Observer demonstrating the effectiveness of this approach despite obstacles from state and local governments attempting to restrict access to digital records.

stories.headline: Easing the fear of a financial collapse
stories.summary: A New Jersey journalist created a bank safety ranking system using federal deposit insurance data to ease consumer fears following the 1991 Bank of New England collapse, helping residents evaluate the security of their local financial institutions.
